_about: Sample threat models for ThreatGuard's offline (rules-only, no API key) 'New Threat Model' flow. Ten example features: five you paste into the 'Describe in text' tab (system_text), and five in the 'Structured' tab (structured_input). Optionally group them under a release to exercise the Release -> Feature -> Threat Model hierarchy.

_usage.text_samples: New Threat Model -> 'Describe in text' -> paste `system_text` -> Create & analyze. API: POST /api/extract-from-text {"text": ...} then POST /api/threat-models.
_usage.structured_samples: New Threat Model -> 'Structured' -> paste `structured_input` -> Create & analyze. API: POST /api/extract-structured {"text": ...}. Grammar: 'Name : type' per line for components; 'A -> B : protocol, auth, encrypted|plaintext' for flows; '#'/blank lines ignored.
_usage.statuses: draft | in_review | in_sprint | released | cancelled; target_date is optional (YYYY-MM-DD).

release.name: 2026 Platform Hardening
release.description: Optional parent release grouping the sample features.

features.name: User Authentication & SSO
features.description: Threat focus: credential stuffing, token theft, session fixation, weak MFA.
features.system_text: Customers sign in to a React web app with email and password or via Google and Okta single sign-on. The web app calls a REST API that validates credentials against an authentication service and issues JWT access tokens with refresh-token rotation. Sessions and rate-limit counters are stored in a Redis cache, and user profiles live in a PostgreSQL database.

features.name: Payment Checkout Flow
features.description: Threat focus: PCI scope, amount tampering, payment-intent replay, webhook spoofing.
features.system_text: Shoppers check out from a web storefront that sends orders to a checkout API behind an API gateway. The checkout service tokenizes card details through the Stripe payment API and never stores card numbers. Orders are written to a PostgreSQL database, and payment webhooks from Stripe are received by a worker that updates order status.

features.name: Admin Console & RBAC
features.description: Threat focus: privilege escalation, IDOR, missing authorization, audit-log tampering.
features.system_text: Internal staff use an admin console, reachable only through a bastion host, to manage refunds and user roles. The console authenticates via SSO against an identity provider, enforces role-based access, reads and writes the accounts PostgreSQL database, and records every action to an append-only audit log.

features.name: Notification Service
features.description: Threat focus: template/HTML injection, notification spoofing, PII leakage, enumeration.
features.system_text: A notification service fans out transactional messages: emails through SendGrid, text messages through an SMS gateway, and mobile push notifications. Application services publish events to a Kafka queue, a worker renders templates, and a secrets manager holds the third-party API keys.

features.name: File Upload & Media Storage
features.description: Threat focus: unrestricted upload, path traversal, content-type confusion, malware hosting.
features.system_text: Users upload profile images and documents from a web app. Files are sent with pre-signed URLs directly to an S3 object store, scanned by a worker for malware, and served back through a CDN. File metadata is kept in a PostgreSQL database.

features.name: Public REST API & Webhooks
features.description: Threat focus: broken object-level authz, rate-limit bypass, SSRF via webhook URLs, key leakage.
features.structured_input:
Client : external_entity
API Gateway : api_gateway
REST API : api
Auth Service : auth_service
Postgres : database
Webhook Dispatcher : worker

Client -> API Gateway : https, api_key, encrypted
API Gateway -> REST API : https, jwt, encrypted
REST API -> Auth Service : https, oauth, encrypted
REST API -> Postgres : sql, password, encrypted
REST API -> Webhook Dispatcher : tcp, none, plaintext
Webhook Dispatcher -> Client : https, none, encrypted

features.name: Mobile App Backend
features.description: Threat focus: insecure deep links, device-token replay, weak transport validation, root/jailbreak abuse.
features.structured_input:
Mobile App : mobile_app
API Gateway : api_gateway
Identity Provider : identity_provider
Profile Service : service
Users DB : database
Push Service : notification_service

Mobile App -> API Gateway : https, jwt, encrypted
API Gateway -> Identity Provider : https, oauth, encrypted
API Gateway -> Profile Service : https, mtls, encrypted
Profile Service -> Users DB : sql, password, encrypted
Profile Service -> Push Service : https, api_key, encrypted

features.name: Data Export & Reporting
features.description: Threat focus: CSV formula injection, over-broad exposure, insecure temp storage, cross-tenant leakage.
features.structured_input:
Analyst : user
Reporting UI : webapp
Export Service : service
Object Store : object_storage
Data Warehouse : data_warehouse
Scheduler : scheduler
Export Worker : worker

Analyst -> Reporting UI : https, sso, encrypted
Reporting UI -> Export Service : https, jwt, encrypted
Scheduler -> Export Worker : https, none, encrypted
Export Worker -> Data Warehouse : sql, password, encrypted
Export Worker -> Object Store : https, iam, encrypted
Reporting UI -> Object Store : https, iam, encrypted

features.name: Third-party OAuth Integrations
features.description: Threat focus: token scope creep, secret storage, connect-flow CSRF, confused deputy.
features.structured_input:
User : user
Web App : webapp
Integration Service : service
Secrets Manager : secrets_manager
GitHub : external_entity
Slack : external_entity
Token DB : database

User -> Web App : https, session, encrypted
Web App -> Integration Service : https, jwt, encrypted
Integration Service -> GitHub : https, oauth, encrypted
Integration Service -> Slack : https, oauth, encrypted
Integration Service -> Secrets Manager : https, iam, encrypted
Integration Service -> Token DB : sql, password, encrypted

features.name: Search & Indexing Service
features.description: Threat focus: query injection, cross-tenant index access, expensive-query DoS, stale ACLs.
features.structured_input:
User : user
Web App : webapp
Search API : api
Search Cluster : search_service
Indexer : worker
Primary DB : database
Events Queue : queue

User -> Web App : https, session, encrypted
Web App -> Search API : https, jwt, encrypted
Search API -> Search Cluster : https, basic, encrypted
Primary DB -> Events Queue : tcp, none, plaintext
Events Queue -> Indexer : tcp, none, plaintext
Indexer -> Search Cluster : https, basic, encrypted
